compiled_state.title: Integration test: FP fallback engages mid-workflow without retroactive effect on existing events
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## TDD Requirement
This is a RED integration test task. Write `test_fp_fallback_mid_workflow` before T11 implementation is complete.

Given: standard/deep PRECONDITIONS events already written for ticket X; FP rate then crosses 10% threshold
When: FP rate tracker engages fallback for ticket X (via fp-rate-tracker.sh)
Then:
  (a) Already-written standard/deep events for ticket X still validate (validators accept them — depth-agnostic)
  (b) New writes on ticket X emit minimal-tier (fallback_engaged marker present in next event)
  (c) Validators continue to accept all depth tiers regardless of fallback state

This test exercises the 3-part done definition for the integration test.

## Implementation Notes
Create `tests/scripts/test-fp-mid-workflow-integration.sh`:

### Setup
1. Create a temp TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR with a synthetic ticket
2. Write a standard-tier PRECONDITIONS event for the ticket (pre-fallback)
3. Write a deep-tier PRECONDITIONS event for the ticket (pre-fallback)

### Engage Fallback
4. Run: `bash plugins/dso/scripts/fp-rate-tracker.sh --ticket-id=<test-ticket> --fp-count=15 --total-events=100`
5. Assert FALLBACK_ENGAGED signal emitted

### Verify (a): existing events still validate
6. Assert the standard-tier event written in step 2 passes depth-agnostic validator check
   - In absence of real validator (dependencies not yet complete), assert that the validator's documented behavior is structural: test that validator does NOT reject standard events based on FALLBACK_ENGAGED state file

### Verify (b): new writes use minimal tier + fallback marker
7. Check that after fallback engaged, classifier should emit minimal tier:
   - Use fp-rate-tracker.sh --query to confirm fallback active
   - Assert --query exits 0 for the affected ticket

### Verify (c): validators depth-agnostic regardless of fallback
8. Verify fp-auto-fallback-scope.md contract explicitly states validators stay depth-agnostic
   - grep for "validators stay depth-agnostic\|depth.agnostic.*fallback" in contract doc

Update `.test-index`:
`plugins/dso/scripts/fp-rate-tracker.sh: tests/scripts/test-fp-fallback-scope-contract.sh [test_fp_rate_tracker_engages_fallback], tests/scripts/test-fp-mid-workflow-integration.sh [test_fp_fallback_mid_workflow]`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Integration test file exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-fp-mid-workflow-integration.sh
- [ ] .test-index entry present for integration test
  Verify: grep '\[test_fp_fallback_mid_workflow\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] Integration test passes (all 3 assertions: a, b, c)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-fp-mid-workflow-integration.sh

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately
